messages.content: I'm prepping for PM interviews this month.
Nice—most guides cover product sense, behavioral stories, and estimation drills. Pick one weakness to stress-test first.
How should I tackle that last part?
What's the difference between behavioral and case interviews?
Behavioral questions probe past actions; case interviews ask you to structure and solve a live business problem out loud.
Show me what the second type looks like in practice.
I get nervous at the start of interviews.
Opening with a clear self-introduction buys you time and frames the conversation—hook, motivation, and a couple of proof points.
What structure does the book suggest for that?
I'm new to tech and keep hearing job titles I don't understand.
Totally normal—many books start by defining the PM role versus project or program managers.
What exactly is the first one?
I have onsite interviews next week.
Good luck—have crisp stories ready and a point of view on why this company fits you.
How do I answer why I want to work here?
